@@ -32,4 +32,3 @@ - * A {@link Value value}, one of {@link Block.Parameter} or {@link Op.Result}, has a - * {@code CodeType} classifying that value. - * A {@link Body} has a {@code CodeType}, the {@link Body#yieldType yield type}, - * classifying values yielded from the body. + * A {@link Value value}, one of {@link Block.Parameter} or {@link Op.Result}, has a {@code CodeType} classifying that + * value. A {@link Body} has a {@code CodeType}, the {@link Body#yieldType yield type}, classifying values yielded from + * the body. @@ -39,0 +38,31 @@ + *

Code type implementation requirements

+ *

+ * Instances of a concrete code type class must be immutable. + *

+ * A concrete code type class must satisfy the following requirements: + *

    + *
  • + * implement {@link #equals(Object)}, {@link #hashCode()}, and {@link #toString()} so that their results are solely + * computed from code type state, and not from an instance's identity; + *
  • + * treat equal instances as freely substitutable, so that interchanging two instances that are equal according to + * {@code equals} produces no visible change in the behavior of the code type's methods; + *
  • + * ensure equal instances have equal {@link #externalize() externalized content}; + *
  • + * provide no creation mechanism that promises unique identity for created instances; + *
  • + * copy mutable constructor arguments that define code type state, ensuring they are all fixed when construction + * completes; and + *
  • + * return unmodifiable views or immutable values from accessors that expose code type state. + *
+ *

+ * A concrete code type class may additionally: + *

    + *
  • + * override {@link #externalize()} to define an external form; and + *
  • + * provide accessors for code type state. + *
+ * @@ -40,2 +70,6 @@ - * Code types enable reasoning statically about a code model, approximating - * run time behavior. + * Code types enable reasoning statically about a code model, approximating run time behavior. + *

+ * A code type might model a Java primitive type such as {@link jdk.incubator.code.dialect.java.JavaType#INT int}, + * a specific Java class such as {@link jdk.incubator.code.dialect.java.JavaType#J_L_STRING String}, or more generally a + * {@link jdk.incubator.code.dialect.core.FunctionType function type} or a + * {@link jdk.incubator.code.dialect.core.TupleType tuple type}. @@ -55,0 +89,4 @@ + * @implSpec + * This implementation returns an externalized code type whose identifier is the result of invoking + * {@link #toString()} and whose argument list is empty. + * @@ -57,1 +95,3 @@ - ExternalizedCodeType externalize(); + default ExternalizedCodeType externalize() { + return ExternalizedCodeType.of(toString()); + } @@ -61,0 +101,4 @@ + *

+ * An implementing class should avoid implementing this method by returning the result of + * {@code externalize().toString()} unless that class overrides the {@code externalize} method with its own + * implementation.

@@ -88,1 +88,2 @@
- * the operation is placed as the root of a code model.
+ * the operation is placed as the root of a code model. After building finishes, the operation's placement and location
+ * are also fixed, and from then on the operation's observable state does not change.
